name: TOGAF Architecture Development Method Ontology
description: TOGAF ADM methodology ontology covering the complete Architecture Development Method lifecycle: ADM phases (Preliminary through Phase H plus Requirements Management), architecture artifact types (Catalogs, Matrices, Diagrams, Deliverables), building blocks (ABB/SBB), requirements, stakeholders, transition architectures, and work packages. Provides the enterprise architecture methodology framework used alongside EA-CORE-ONT domain entities.

oaa:imports.name: EA-CORE-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Platform-agnostic EA core providing base entity types and architecture domains

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Phase-to-Domain Mapping
oaa:joinPatterns.description: TOGAF ADM phases refine EA-CORE architecture domains through structured development
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Architecture development progress tracking across domains

entities.name: ADMPhase
entities.description: A phase in the TOGAF Architecture Development Method cycle. The ADM consists of Preliminary Phase, Phases A through H, and Requirements Management, forming an iterative cycle for enterprise architecture development.

oaa:properties.name: objective
oaa:properties.description: Primary objective of this ADM phase

oaa:properties.name: keyActivities
oaa:properties.description: Key activities performed during this phase

entities.name: ArchitectureDomain
entities.description: One of the four TOGAF architecture domains (Business, Data, Application, Technology) that structure the enterprise architecture into complementary views, each containing specific catalogs, matrices, and diagrams.

entities.name: ArchitectureArtifact
entities.description: A work product produced during architecture development. Artifacts include catalogs, matrices, diagrams, and deliverables that document the architecture at various levels of abstraction and from different viewpoints.

entities.name: Catalog
entities.description: A list of building blocks of a specific type, providing an inventory of elements within an architecture domain (e.g., Business Capability Catalog, Application Portfolio Catalog, Technology Standards Catalog).

entities.name: Matrix
entities.description: A grid showing relationships between building blocks across different domains or within a domain (e.g., Application/Data Matrix, Stakeholder Map Matrix, Application/Technology Matrix).

entities.name: Diagram
entities.description: A visual representation of building blocks and their relationships, providing graphical views of the architecture (e.g., Business Capability Map, Application Communication Diagram, Platform Decomposition Diagram).

entities.name: Deliverable
entities.description: A contractually specified work product that is formally reviewed, agreed, and signed off by stakeholders. Deliverables are produced by ADM phases and consumed by subsequent phases.

entities.name: BuildingBlock
entities.description: A potentially reusable component of enterprise capability that can be combined with other building blocks to deliver architectures and solutions. Includes Architecture Building Blocks (ABBs) and Solution Building Blocks (SBBs).

oaa:properties.name: buildingBlockType
oaa:properties.description: Whether this is an ABB or SBB

entities.name: Requirement
entities.description: A statement of need that must be met by the architecture. Requirements are managed throughout the ADM cycle via Requirements Management and traced to architecture artifacts and building blocks.

oaa:properties.name: priority
oaa:properties.description: Requirement priority: MoSCoW (Must, Should, Could, Won't)

oaa:properties.name: status
oaa:properties.description: Requirement status: Draft, Approved, Implemented, Verified

entities.name: Stakeholder
entities.description: An individual, team, or organisation with interests in or concerns about the architecture. Stakeholders are mapped to concerns and addressed by specific ADM phases.

oaa:properties.name: concern
oaa:properties.description: Primary concern or interest of this stakeholder

oaa:properties.name: influence
oaa:properties.description: Level of influence: Low, Medium, High

entities.name: TransitionArchitecture
entities.description: An intermediate architecture state between baseline and target that defines a stable, achievable checkpoint during architecture transformation. Multiple transitions may exist on the roadmap.

oaa:properties.name: baselineRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to the baseline architecture state

oaa:properties.name: targetRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to the target architecture state

entities.name: WorkPackage
entities.description: A set of activities and deliverables for implementing architecture changes. Work packages are the implementation units that bridge architecture planning to project execution.

oaa:properties.name: estimatedEffort
oaa:properties.description: Estimated effort for the work package

relationships.name: precedes
relationships.description: An ADM phase precedes another phase in the development cycle, establishing the sequential flow of the ADM iteration

relationships.name: follows
relationships.description: An ADM phase follows another phase in the development cycle, the inverse of precedes

relationships.name: produces
relationships.description: An ADM phase produces architecture artifacts as outputs, establishing the deliverable chain through the ADM cycle

relationships.name: consumes
relationships.description: An ADM phase consumes architecture artifacts as inputs from previous phases or the architecture repository

relationships.name: governsPhase
relationships.description: An architecture artifact provides governance over ADM phases, constraining and guiding phase execution

relationships.name: validates
relationships.description: An ADM phase validates architecture artifacts to confirm conformance and fitness for purpose

relationships.name: informs
relationships.description: An architecture domain informs another domain, establishing the information flow between Business, Data, Application, and Technology domains

relationships.name: refines
relationships.description: An architecture artifact refines another artifact, providing more detail or a more specific version

relationships.name: contains
relationships.description: An architecture artifact or domain contains other artifacts, establishing compositional hierarchies

relationships.name: realizesRequirement
relationships.description: A building block realises one or more architecture requirements, providing the capability that satisfies the stated need

relationships.name: addresses
relationships.description: An ADM phase addresses stakeholder concerns, ensuring stakeholder needs are considered during architecture development

relationships.name: tracesTo
relationships.description: An architecture artifact traces to requirements for traceability from design decisions to stated needs

enumerations.name: ADMPhaseCode
enumerations.description: Standard TOGAF ADM phase identifiers covering the full development cycle

enumerations.name: ArtifactType
enumerations.description: Classification of TOGAF architecture artifact types

enumerations.name: BuildingBlockType
enumerations.description: Classification of TOGAF building block types

hasDefinedTerm.name: ArchitectureBuildingBlock
hasDefinedTerm.description: Describes required capability at architecture level

hasDefinedTerm.name: SolutionBuildingBlock
hasDefinedTerm.description: Implements an ABB with specific products/technologies

businessRules.name: ADMPhaseSequencing
businessRules.description: Each ADM phase must establish sequential flow by having a precedes relationship or being the cycle endpoint
businessRules.condition: IF ea-togaf:ADMPhase exists and phaseCode is not 'H'
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one ea-togaf:precedes relationship to the next phase

businessRules.name: PhaseProducesArtifacts
businessRules.description: Every ADM phase must produce at least one architecture artifact as its output
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ea-togaf:produces relationship to an ea-togaf:ArchitectureArtifact

businessRules.name: CatalogDomainContainment
businessRules.description: Catalogs must be contained within an architecture domain for proper classification
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST be referenced by an ea-togaf:ArchitectureDomain via ea-togaf:contains

businessRules.name: SBBRealizesABB
businessRules.description: Solution Building Blocks must realise at least one Architecture Building Block
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ea-togaf:realizesRequirement relationship

businessRules.name: TransitionArchitectureStates
businessRules.description: Transition architectures must reference both baseline and target states for gap clarity
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have non-empty baselineRef and targetRef properties

businessRules.name: DeliverablePhaseProduction
businessRules.description: Deliverables must be produced by exactly one ADM phase for clear accountability
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST be referenced by exactly one ea-togaf:ADMPhase via ea-togaf:produces

businessRules.name: WorkPackageDeliverableAssociation
businessRules.description: Work packages must contain at least one deliverable to define their scope
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ea-togaf:contains relationship to an ea-togaf:Deliverable

businessRules.name: RequirementPhaseTraceability
businessRules.description: Requirements must be traceable to at least one ADM phase that consumes them
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST be referenced by at least one ea-togaf:ADMPhase via ea-togaf:consumes

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial creation from EA-ONT V3 source consolidation. TOGAF ADM methodology extracted from togaf-adm-extension-v2-pre-oaav5, covering 10 ADM phases, artifact taxonomy, building blocks, and governance lifecycle. Core entity types retained; individual deliverable instances available as separate instance data.

versionHistory.notes: Initial release: TOGAF ADM ontology from V3 source consolidation
